sections.title: Manifest bootstrap — wire the pool catalog into the app
sections.key_moment: The pure `parseManifest` validator. Decoupling validation from filesystem I/O is what lets the same function back the Node-side fs loader AND the browser-side fetch loader; it is the single piece the learner should write themselves.

sections.title: RPC foundation — read a pool's inner state via sui_getObject
sections.key_moment: The descent through `Versioned<PoolInner>` wrappers. The pool object's `content.fields.value.fields` path is the non-obvious bit — get it wrong and everything downstream returns undefined.

sections.title: Order book traversal — decode the BigVector slices
sections.key_moment: Mapping an `order_id` to its price. The high bit flags ask vs bid; bits 64–127 hold the price scaled by `tick_size`. Bid prices are inverted in the order_id encoding — the data layer prefers the price field from the companion OrderInfo event to dodge that decoder ambiguity entirely.

sections.title: Pure market-stats math — mid, spread, depth
sections.key_moment: The depth-within-1% calculation. It's the only piece that requires both sides of the book at once; getting the price-band boundary right (best_bid * 0.99 ≤ p ≤ best_ask * 1.01) is the bit the learner should produce themselves.

sections.title: Fills pagination + 24h aggregation
sections.key_moment: The pagination stop condition: bail out when the page's oldest timestamp falls past the 24h window OR when 50 fills have been collected, whichever comes first. Without it `suix_queryEvents` will happily drain the entire history.

sections.title: App wiring — graceful per-pool failures + 20s refresh
sections.key_moment: `Promise.allSettled` + a per-pool sentinel card. One transient pool error must not blank the whole dashboard — the dashboard is more useful with an `—` somewhere than with a blank screen.
